Package com.rays.config

Class JWTUtil

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.rays.config.JWTUtil
  • @Component
public class JWTUtil
extends java.lang.Object
    Utility class for generating, validating, and extracting claims from JSON Web Tokens (JWT) in the ORS application.

    Uses HMAC-SHA256 (HS256) for signing tokens and Base64 URL encoding for header and payload serialization. Token secret and expiration duration are injected from application properties.

    Author:
    Krati Trivedi

      Computes an HMAC-SHA256 signature for the given data using the provided key.

    • Field Detail

      • jwtSecret

        @Value("${jwt.secret}")
private java.lang.String jwtSecret
        The secret key used for signing and validating JWT tokens. Injected from the jwt.secret application property.

      • jwtExpiration

        @Value("${jwt.expiration}")
private long jwtExpiration
        The token expiration duration in milliseconds. Injected from the jwt.expiration application property.

      • objectMapper

        private static final com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper objectMapper
        Shared ObjectMapper instance used for JSON serialization and deserialization of JWT header and payload.

    • Constructor Detail

      • JWTUtil

        public JWTUtil()

    • Method Detail

      • generateToken

        public java.lang.String generateToken​(java.lang.Long userId,
                                      java.lang.String loginId,
                                      java.lang.String role)
                               throws java.lang.Exception
        Generates a signed JWT token for the given user details.

        The token contains the following claims in the payload: subject (loginId), userId, role, issued-at timestamp, and expiration timestamp. The token is signed using HMAC-SHA256 and returned as a Base64 URL-encoded string.

        Parameters:
        userId - the unique identifier of the user
        loginId - the login ID (typically email) of the user, used as the JWT subject
        role - the role assigned to the user
        Returns:
        a signed JWT token string in the format header.payload.signature
        Throws:
        java.lang.Exception - if an error occurs during JSON serialization or HMAC signing

      • validateToken

        public boolean validateToken​(java.lang.String token,
                             java.lang.String expectedLoginId)
                      throws java.lang.Exception
        Validates the given JWT token against the expected login ID.

        Performs the following checks:

        • Token structure must contain exactly three parts
        • Signature must match the recomputed HMAC-SHA256 signature
        • Subject (loginId) must match the provided expectedLoginId
        • Token must not be expired

        Parameters:
        token - the JWT token string to validate
        expectedLoginId - the expected login ID to match against the token subject
        Returns:
        true if the token is valid
        Throws:
        java.lang.Exception - if the token is invalid, tampered with, or expired

      • extractLoginId

        public java.lang.String extractLoginId​(java.lang.String token)
        Extracts the login ID (subject claim) from the given JWT token.
        Parameters:
        token - the JWT token string
        Returns:
        the login ID stored in the sub claim

      • extractUserId

        public java.lang.Long extractUserId​(java.lang.String token)
        Extracts the user ID from the given JWT token.
        Parameters:
        token - the JWT token string
        Returns:
        the user ID stored in the userId claim as a Long

      • extractRole

        public java.lang.String extractRole​(java.lang.String token)
        Extracts the role from the given JWT token.
        Parameters:
        token - the JWT token string
        Returns:
        the role stored in the role claim as a String

      • isTokenExpired

        private boolean isTokenExpired​(java.lang.String payloadJson)
        Checks whether the given JWT payload indicates an expired token.

        Compares the exp claim value against the current system time in seconds.

        Parameters:
        payloadJson - the decoded JSON string of the JWT payload
        Returns:
        true if the token has expired, false otherwise

      • extractField

        private java.lang.String extractField​(java.lang.String json,
                                      java.lang.String field)
        Extracts the value of a specific field from a JSON string.
        Parameters:
        json - the JSON string to parse
        field - the field name whose value is to be extracted
        Returns:
        the field value as a String, or null if parsing fails

      • sign

        private java.lang.String sign​(java.lang.String data,
                              java.lang.String key)
                       throws java.lang.Exception
        Computes an HMAC-SHA256 signature for the given data using the provided key.
        Parameters:
        data - the input string to sign
        key - the secret key used for signing
        Returns:
        the Base64 URL-encoded signature string
        Throws:
        java.lang.Exception - if the HMAC-SHA256 algorithm is unavailable or the key is invalid

      • encodeUrl

        private java.lang.String encodeUrl​(java.lang.String data)
        Encodes the given string to a Base64 URL-encoded string without padding.
        Parameters:
        data - the string to encode
        Returns:
        the Base64 URL-encoded representation of the input string

      • encodeUrl

        private java.lang.String encodeUrl​(byte[] data)
        Encodes the given byte array to a Base64 URL-encoded string without padding.
        Parameters:
        data - the byte array to encode
        Returns:
        the Base64 URL-encoded representation of the input byte array

      • decode

        private java.lang.String decode​(java.lang.String data)
        Decodes a Base64 URL-encoded string back to its original UTF-8 string representation.
        Parameters:
        data - the Base64 URL-encoded string to decode
        Returns:
        the decoded UTF-8 string
